Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We need to produce 20 open ended questions about the described supply chain process

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: They should be thought provoking focusing on insights potential issues strategies for improvement implications of decisions No SQL queries Just list the questions

Name: Validation - PE
Text: We must adhere to formatting plain text The instructions did not specify formatting beyond not providing SQL queries

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: So we can just list numbered or bullet points

Name: Validation - PE
Text: We should ensure no mention of model or anything else Just the questions

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Lets craft 20 well thought out open ended questions covering RD design decisions supplier lead times and risk management manufacturing scheduling and shift optimization logistics coordination across modes and regions inventory strategy JIT vs aggregated demand forecasting accuracy with ML models regulatory compliance across multiple jurisdictions environmental impact and carbon footprint reduction strategies quality assurance protocols and recall processes digital system integration challenges ERP SCM currency risk management in procurement and sales pricing strategies

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Also ask about resilience to disruptions like pandemics or geopolitical tensions how data analytics informs decision making how after sales support is integrated into supply chain planning how product lifecycle management influences component sourcing

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Lets produce a numbered list of 20 questions Ensure each question is open ended How might What are the implications of etc
